;<span id="Hook_node">hook node</span>
:place that determines where new [[Glossary:Element|elements]] are added in the [[Glossary:Contents_window|'''Contents''' window]] (e.g. with '''[[Add new]]'''). A '''hook''' is a [[Glossary:Node|knowledge tree node]] to which new [[Glossary:Element|elements]] are added (as [[Glossary:Child|children]]). The '''hook node''' is either the same as the [[Glossary:Root_node|root node]], or is a [[Glossary:Child|child]] of the [[Glossary:Root node|root node]], or is a [[Glossary:Descendant|descendant]] of the [[Glossary:Root_node|root node]]. Once the [[SuperMemo tab in Options#limit|children limit]] of a '''hook''' is passed, a new '''hook''' is chosen. The newly chosen '''hook''' is always a [[Glossary:Descendant|descendant]] of the [[Glossary:Root_node|root]] of the current [[Glossary:Concept_group|concept group]]. If [[Glossary:SuperMemo|SuperMemo]] runs out of [[Glossary:Branch|branches]] that might become new '''hooks''', it will restructure the concept [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] and start a new level of branches under the [[Glossary:Root_node|root node]] with new space for new '''hooks''' and new [[Glossary:Element|elements]].
